statement.en: Using ASP(Q) to Handle Inconsistent Prioritized Data
statement.summary: We explore the use of answer set programming (ASP) and its extension with quantifiers, ASP(Q), for inconsistency-tolerant querying of prioritized data, where a priority relation between conflicting facts is exploited to define three notions of optimal repairs (Pareto-, globally- and completion-optimal). We consider the variants of three well-known semantics (AR, brave and IAR) that use these optimal repairs, and for which query answering is in the first or second level of the polynomial hierarchy for a large class of logical theories. Notably, this paper presents the first implementation of globally-optimal repair-based semantics, as well as the first implementation of the grounded semantics, which is a tractable under-approximation of all these optimal repair-based semantics. Our experime

proposer: Meghyn Bienvenu, Camille Bourgaux, Robin Jean et al.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Paper describes novel implementation of theoretical semantics (globally-optimal repair, grounded semantics) for inconsistency-tolerant querying. Claims are provisional pending peer review validation; methods are sound but experimental results incomplete in abstract. Status: preprint (2026-04-25), not yet formally published/confirmed.
